faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Claude Code questions worth answering before you start
faq.description: A compact version of the original homepage FAQ, updated for the new knowledge base: quick answers first, then deeper guides when the question deserves a full page.

items.question: What is Claude Code 101?
items.answer: Claude Code 101 is a practical search hub and tutorial knowledge base for Claude Code. It helps developers move from quick questions like installation, pricing, usage limits, and MCP setup into deeper guides on CLAUDE.md, Plan Mode, workflows, and troubleshooting.

items.question: How should I install Claude Code?
items.answer: Start with the current official installation path, confirm that the claude command works in your terminal, then open a small project before giving Claude Code broad tool access. The installation guide and FAQ pages keep this flow separated by platform and common setup issues.

items.question: Is Claude Code free, and how do pricing or limits work?
items.answer: Pricing, plan access, and usage limits can change, so this site avoids copying stale tables. Use the pricing and limits pages to understand what affects cost, then verify the final plan details against official Anthropic sources.

items.question: What is CLAUDE.md used for?
items.answer: CLAUDE.md is the durable project context file for Claude Code. Put commands, architecture notes, coding standards, test rules, and delivery expectations there so repeated sessions start with the same working agreement.

items.question: Do I need MCP servers on day one?
items.answer: No. Use MCP when a real workflow needs external tools such as GitHub, browser automation, docs lookup, or project services. The MCP pages focus on choosing fewer useful servers, limiting permissions, and checking security before expanding access.

items.question: Where should I start when Claude Code fails?
items.answer: Begin with the error shape: command not found, login or API key problems, permission errors, rate limits, connection failures, or MCP not connecting. The troubleshooting section turns those into focused fix paths instead of one long generic checklist.
